name: VERA full-certification plug-in learning-curve diagnostic
purpose: Compare observed VERA-IUT abstention across the five locked certification fractions with a full-certification empirical plug-in prediction. This is a calibration diagnostic, not independent validation of the finite-sample theorem.

bootstrap.sampling_unit: Aligned certification example across all candidates and attackers.
bootstrap.strata: Cartesian cells of target label, binary source label, and registered environment.
bootstrap.procedure: Within each dataset and algorithmic seed, draw one full-length empirical-bootstrap sequence with replacement inside every stratum. For each fraction, take the registered max(1, floor(fraction times stratum size)) prefix from every stratum. Prefixes are nested within each replicate.
bootstrap.dependence: The same sampled indices are applied to all 12 candidates and all four attackers, preserving their row-wise dependence. Bootstrap replicate indices are coordinated across the eight algorithmic seeds before aggregation.
bootstrap.certificate: Recompute the fixed-profile balanced VERA intersection-union test at candidate alpha delta divided by 12. Paired target bounds split candidate alpha across positive and negative tails; each balanced leakage bound splits it across the two source-class recalls.
bootstrap.aggregation: For each dataset, fraction, and bootstrap replicate, average abstention across eight seeds and all nine locked threshold pairs.
bootstrap.interval: Pointwise 95 percent percentile interval over 2000 bootstrap aggregate abstention rates.
bootstrap.observed_curve: Use the deterministic nested stratified subsets fixed in the parent preregistration and the receipt-backed VERA-IUT rule rows.

reporting.primary_plot: For each dataset, overlay observed abstention at all five fractions on the plug-in bootstrap mean and pointwise 95 percent band.
reporting.coverage_summary: Report each point as inside or outside its band and the number of datasets for which all five observed points are inside.
reporting.support_mismatch: Camelyon17 VERA abstention is fixed to one in both observed and predicted curves because external center 2 is unsupported. Display it but do not treat it as evidence that the bootstrap predicts statistical power.
reporting.claim_boundary: The full certification fold estimates the empirical law used by the bootstrap, so this overlay is descriptive calibration. Only the separately locked synthetic law provides an exact theory-data prediction.
